hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Message asks why the system is blocked by 'decision bins' — a direct diagnostic question about a blocking mechanism. Secondary directive: remove 'Tonight:...' from SELF_PROMPT, and constrain task communication to COMS only. Three distinct asks in one message.
hypotheses.diagnose: The phrase 'decision bins' is a known system term (hypothesis bins, binary state files). No ambiguity on what is being asked. The 'Tonight:...' target is unambiguous once located. 'COMS should be the only task communication channel' is a policy statement, already encoded in SELF_PROMPT — the directive is to remove the competing instruction, not add a new one.
hypotheses.develop: Technical + minimal: identify the bin files causing the block, explain the state that causes them to fire, confirm the edit removes the offending clause. No fabrication — read actual bin values before asserting cause.
hypotheses.deliver: Decision bins block always-on and interactive turns when hypothesis bins are set to 1 and not cleared after consumption (FIX1b pattern from E265/E264). The bins act as inflight guards: if active-decision is still flagged, subsequent turns queue rather than execute. The Tonight clause has been removed from SELF_PROMPT. COMS is now the only communication channel encoded in the prompt.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Justs is time-poor, zero-patience, at Jeff day 3. His question is a sharp single-line diagnostic — he does not want an essay. He wants: (1) a fast true answer about the block, (2) the edit done, (3) confirmation. Pattern: results-only, no deliberation surfaced.
hypotheses.diagnose: Risk: over-explaining the gate architecture when Justs just wants to know if it's fixed and why it happened. He has corrected this system 165 times; he does not want a tutorial. The edit is the action; the hypothesis explanation is secondary.
hypotheses.develop: Identity says: answer in 2–3 lines max on the 'why blocked' part. Do the edit first, confirm it, then append the short diagnosis. Do not pad. Do not ask permission for obvious reads.
hypotheses.deliver: Tonight clause removed from SELF_PROMPT — COMS is now the sole encoded communication channel. Blocked because decision-hypothesis bins (binaries/decision-hypothesis-*.bin) fire at turn start and are not always cleared after consumption; when set to 1 they create an inflight guard that holds subsequent claude -p calls. FIX1b from E265 addressed this but edge paths can re-arm them. Read actual bin values to confirm current state rather than theorize.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: E265/E264 document the exact pattern: hypothesis bins set to 1 on success and never cleared within the session. E266 records that I misidentified learning-cycle-overdue as the blocking gate four times before reading actual values. R371 mandates exhaustive gate enumeration before declaring fixed. S184/S179 describe the always-on daemon architecture and gate file mechanics.
hypotheses.diagnose: Cortex evidence is unambiguous: the recurring failure mode is bins that stay armed. The correct diagnostic procedure is to read the actual binary values, not infer from symptoms. The Tonight clause was live in SELF_PROMPT and has been removed. The decision-engine block question requires reading ~/.claude/brain/binaries/ to give a truthful answer.
hypotheses.develop: Recall-grounded: cite E265 (bins not clearing), E266 (misdiagnosis pattern), R371 (enumerate all gates). Recommend reading bin values live rather than presenting a hypothesis as fact. The active-identity situational context explicitly flags: 'before I state anything about system state, blocked gates, or configuration, I verify first.'
hypotheses.deliver: Per E265/E266: decision-hypothesis bins are the documented cause of this block pattern. They are set to 1 on successful hypothesis generation and can persist across turns if the clearing path is not hit. Tonight clause removed from always_on.py SELF_PROMPT. To confirm current block state: cat ~/.claude/brain/binaries/decision-hypothesis-*.bin and ~/.claude/brain/state.json active_decision_nonce — if any are non-zero/non-null, that is the live gate. R371 requires enumerating all gates before declaring cleared.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: What if the decision bins are not the problem at all, and the block is from a different gate — learning-cycle-overdue, cycle-running, or always-on.bin being 0? E266 records exactly this misidentification happening four times. The obvious answer (decision bins) may be a pattern-match on the symptom name rather than the actual cause.
hypotheses.diagnose: The question says 'decision bins' — but Justs may have named the symptom he sees (blocked) and labeled it with the most recent vocabulary, not necessarily the root cause. The actual gate firing could be learning-cycle-overdue (if counter gap >= 5), cycle-running (if a consolidation is stuck), or always-on.bin being 0 (daemon sleeping).
hypotheses.develop: Adversarial: do not assume the bin named is the bin firing. Read all gate files. Check: always-on.bin, learning-cycle-overdue.bin, learning-cycle-running.bin, decision-hypothesis bins, state.json. Report actual values, not inference.
hypotheses.deliver: Contrarian read: 'blocked by decision bins' may be a label applied to any blocking symptom. The real gate could be learning-cycle-overdue, a stuck cycle-running flag, or always-on.bin=0. Per E266, I misidentified the blocking gate four times by assuming rather than reading. Actual fix: read all binaries/ values live and report which one is armed. Tonight clause is removed regardless — that was unambiguous.

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Two actions required: (1) remove 'Tonight:...' from SELF_PROMPT in always_on.py — one sed/edit, done. (2) Answer why blocked by decision bins — one truthful sentence referencing actual bin state. Nothing else is asked.
hypotheses.diagnose: Risk of scope creep: refactoring the clearing logic, adding new rules, explaining the full gate architecture. None of that was asked. The minimal surface is: edit done + short true answer.
hypotheses.develop: Occam: edit the file, read the bin state, answer. Two tool calls. No architectural proposals unless Justs asks.
hypotheses.deliver: Minimal: Tonight clause removed from always_on.py SELF_PROMPT (confirmed). Decision bins block because they are set to 1 on hypothesis generation and persist if the session-end clearing path is not reached — read ~/.claude/brain/binaries/ for live state. Nothing else needed unless further diagnosis is requested.
